meta.reportLabel: Tue - NZ Q2 CPI RUNS HOT (4.1% y/y vs 4.0% exp, 1.5% q/q), a 2-year high above the RBNZ's own 3.9% call - NZD/USD to ~0.5865 and back-to-back RBNZ hikes are live. But the internals are fuel: petrol +27.5%, ex-fuel CPI just 2.9%, sectoral model flat at 2.7%. Meanwhile OIL STALLS at the highs (WTI ~$82.4 -0.1%, Brent ~$90.3) as mediators float a 10-DAY CEASEFIRE that would reopen Hormuz - the first credible unwind path in a week. Canada's June CPI cooled hard to 2.8% (trim 1.8%, median 1.9%), so CAD drops from strongest to firm. NZD takes the board. AUD/NZD SHORT (HIGH); NZD/CAD FLIPS to LONG (MED); USOIL cut to RANGE. USD/JPY ~162.5 is now ABOVE the MoF line.
meta.coverage: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, AUD, NZD, CAD · 18 pairs + DXY + JPYBASKET + GER40 + XAU + XAG + USOIL
meta.regime: The oil-premium regime got its first credible two-way in a week: mediators floated a 10-day ceasefire that would explicitly reopen the Strait of Hormuz, and crude went flat at the highs (WTI ~$82.4, Brent ~$90.3) even as the US ran a 10th consecutive night of strikes. That flips the marginal headline risk to the downside for every oil-linked leg. Simultaneously NZ Q2 CPI ran hot at 4.1%, making NZD the day's clean winner, while Canada's June core cooled across all three BoC gauges and knocked CAD off the top.
meta.nextBigEvent: UK June CPI (Wed Jul 22, 06:00Z) then the ECB decision (Thu Jul 23) - the start of three central-bank binaries inside nine days (ECB Jul 23, FOMC Jul 29, BoE Jul 30, BoJ Jul 31), with the 10-day ceasefire proposal running as the dominant two-way risk over all of it.

dailyRead: Two things changed overnight and both cut against Monday's map. First, NZ Q2 CPI printed hot - 4.1% y/y (vs 4.0% exp) and 1.5% q/q (vs 1.4%), the fastest in over two years and above the RBNZ's own 'peaked 3.9%' call, lifting NZD/USD to ~0.5865 and putting back-to-back RBNZ hikes genuinely on the table after the July 8 lift to 2.50%. The caveat matters for sizing: transport rose 10.6% with petrol +27.5%, and ex-petrol/diesel CPI is only 2.9% while the RBNZ's sectoral factor model held at 2.7% - this is an energy pass-through print, not a broad-based one, and the RBNZ can look through it. Second, and bigger for everything else, the oil trade stopped going up: WTI is ~$82.4 (-0.1%) and Brent ~$90.3 with momentum flat, while mediators floated a 10-day ceasefire that would explicitly lift the Strait of Hormuz restrictions - the White House hasn't accepted, and the US is on its 10th consecutive night of strikes, but for the first time in a week the premium has a credible unwind path. Meanwhile Canada's June CPI cooled hard to 2.8% (from 3.2%) with CPI-trim 1.8% and median 1.9%, which quietly undercuts the BoC's conditional-hawkish leg. Net: NZD is the day's clean winner and CAD gets downgraded from strongest to merely firm. Cleanest leans: AUD/NZD SHORT (HIGH) - hot-CPI NZD vs an AUD with loosening labour (4.4%) and no CPI until Jul 29, with the oil/risk legs cancelling; NZD/CAD flips from SHORT to LONG (MED); NZD/JPY and CAD/JPY LONG (MED); USOIL downgraded to RANGE-long tilt on the ceasefire optionality. The standing landmine: USD/JPY at ~162.5 is now above the MoF line, deep in the strike zone.

today.headline: Q2 CPI ran hot at 4.1% y/y (vs 4.0% exp) and 1.5% q/q - fastest in over two years, above the RBNZ's own 3.9% call; NZD/USD up to ~0.5865.
today.read: Back-to-back RBNZ hikes into Aug 19 are now genuinely live, making NZD the board's strongest leg today. But size for the internals: petrol rose 27.5% and ex-fuel CPI is just 2.9% with the sectoral model flat at 2.7% - if officials say they are looking through the fuel component, the hawkish repricing unwinds fast.

today.headline: June CPI cooled hard to 2.8% (from 3.2%) with all three BoC core gauges falling - trim 1.8%, median 1.9%, common 2.6%; the ceasefire proposal threatens the oil premium.
today.read: Downgraded from strongest to firm: the soft core read sits awkwardly with Macklem's 'hikes if oil spikes' framing, and a signed ceasefire would remove the terms-of-trade tailwind outright. AUD/CAD neutralises to RANGE and NZD/CAD flips to LONG.

today.headline: A Reuters poll now shows a growing majority expecting a second 2026 ECB hike in September on the energy resurgence; June HICP confirmed at 2.8%.
today.read: Upgraded from softer to mixed - the same oil shock read as a pure terms-of-trade drag is now the main argument for a September hike. Jul 23 is a hold (~88% priced); the Sept signalling in Lagarde's tone is the entire EUR trade this week.

today.headline: USD/JPY pushed through to ~162.5, now ABOVE the MoF line at a 40-year low; Tokyo core CPI 1.6% and the fuel-stripped trend gauge jumped to 1.9%.
today.read: Still the weakest currency, but the hawkish offset is building from both ends: oil is filtering into Tokyo CPI and hands the BoJ real ammunition at Jul 31, while spot sits inside the intervention strike zone. Keep every XXX/JPY carry-long sized for a violent MoF snap.

today.headline: DXY slipped to ~100.7 and the dollar lost ground to both Antipodeans even as it gained on EUR - the oil-inflation bid flattened with crude.
today.read: The Sept-hike tail (~55-60%) is intact into the Jul 28-29 FOMC but is now explicitly oil-dependent. A ceasefire deflates the inflation scare and re-arms the soft-USD slide; DXY downgraded to a directionless RANGE.

today.headline: No change - unemployment sits at 4.4% (May) and loosening, with Q2 CPI not due until Jul 29.
today.read: That catalyst vacuum is precisely the AUD/NZD short: NZD just got its print, AUD waits nine days. Sticky trimmed mean (3.6%) gives a floor, but the Aug 11 RBA hike tail is thin (~35%) while labour keeps softening.

today.headline: No change - services CPI at 3.7% keeps both BoE hike dissents alive; the June CPI binary lands tomorrow.
today.read: Firm own rate story into Jul 30 (BoE + MPR), with the Bank's own path seeing CPI above 3.25% by Q4. Tomorrow's June CPI decides the dissent count and the near-term cable direction; gilt jitters cap the upside.

strength.drivers: Q2 CPI 4.1% y/y (vs 4.0% exp) and 1.5% q/q, a two-year high above the RBNZ's own 3.9% call; OCR 2.50% after the Jul 8 hike; unemployment 5.3%.
strength.forward: 🦅 The 4.1%/1.5% double beat overshoots the RBNZ's 3.9% call and hands the hawks a mandate for a follow-up hike into Aug 19. But read the internals: transport +10.6%, petrol +27.5%, ex-fuel CPI just 2.9% and the sectoral model unchanged at 2.7% - this is oil pass-through, exactly the kind of print a central bank looks through. Risk: officials explicitly discount the fuel component; a ceasefire crushes petrol; a risk-off flush hits high-beta NZD.

strength.drivers: Core CPI 2.6% (Jun, flat m/m), headline 3.5%; NFP +57k with unemployment 4.2%; Fed 3.50-3.75% on a hawkish hold, Sept-hike odds ~55-60%; DXY ~100.7.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ DXY slipped to ~100.7 and the dollar lost ground to both Antipodeans even as it gained on EUR - the oil-inflation bid that drove last week's firming has flattened with crude. Warsh's 'prices are too high' keeps the Sept-hike tail armed into the Jul 28-29 FOMC (a hold), but the hawkish impulse needs oil to keep rising. Risk: a ceasefire deflates the inflation scare; a hot Aug 12 CPI re-fires it.

strength.drivers: June CPI 2.8% (from 3.2%) with CPI-trim 1.8%, median 1.9%, common 2.6% - all cooled; BoC held 2.25% on Jul 15 for a sixth straight time; jobs +18.2k, unemployment 6.5%.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Two legs weakened at once. June CPI fell to 2.8% with all three core gauges cooling - materially softer than the 'hikes if oil spikes' narrative implies - and the 10-day ceasefire proposal directly threatens the terms-of-trade premium that made CAD the board's leader. Still a net-energy exporter with a real tailwind, but conviction is halved. Risk: a fresh escalation re-spikes crude and restores the lead; a ceasefire removes it entirely.

strength.drivers: Core CPI 2.6% (May) with services at 3.7%; headline 2.8% vs a 2% target; unemployment 4.6%, DMP wages 4.2%; BoE 3.75% on a hawkish 7-2 hold with two hike votes.
strength.forward: 🦅⚖️ Services CPI at 3.7% keeps both hike dissents alive into Jul 30 (BoE + MPR), and the Bank's own path sees CPI a little under 3% in Q3 rising past 3.25% in Q4 - a hawkish structural backdrop. UK June CPI lands Jul 22 and is the immediate binary; gilt jitters are a background drag. Risk: a soft CPI kills the dissents; a risk-off flush hits high-beta GBP.

strength.drivers: June HICP 2.8% (from 3.2%) with core at 2.5%; unemployment 6.3%; ECB deposit rate 2.25% after the Jun 11 hike, meeting-by-meeting guidance; Jul 23 hold ~88% priced.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Upgraded from softer. A Reuters poll now shows a growing majority expecting a second 2026 hike in September, with Commerzbank and Goldman aligned, precisely because the energy resurgence threatens the inflation path - the same oil shock read as a terms-of-trade drag is now also a hike catalyst. Jul 23 is a hold; the tone and any Sept signalling is the whole trade. Risk: HICP back near target undercuts the hawks; a ceasefire removes the energy argument.

strength.drivers: Trimmed mean 3.6% (May) with headline 4.0% above the 2-3% band; unemployment 4.4% (May) and drifting higher; RBA held 4.35% in June after 75bp of 2026 hikes.
strength.forward: 🔻⚖️ Sticky core gives a floor, but unemployment has drifted to 4.4% and the RBA is openly in a tug-of-war between inflation and jobs. Crucially AUD has no domestic catalyst until Q2 CPI on Jul 29 while NZD just got one - that asymmetry is the AUD/NZD trade. Aug 11 hike tail thin (~35%). Risk: a hot Q2 CPI flips it hawkish fast; a ceasefire risk-on melt-up lifts high-betas.

strength.drivers: Tokyo core 1.6% (Jun, up from 1.3%) with the fuel-stripped trend gauge at 1.9% (from 1.6%); headline 1.5% below the 2% target; unemployment 2.5%, jobs/applicants 1.26; BoJ 1.00% after the Jun 16 hike.
strength.forward: ⚖️🦅 Still the oil-shock terms-of-trade loser, and USD/JPY has pushed through to ~162.5 - now above the MoF's line and deep in the strike zone (verbal only so far; the record 11.73tn yen Apr/May intervention failed to hold within six weeks). But the offset is strengthening: Tokyo core jumped to 1.6% and the trend gauge to 1.9% as crude filters through electricity and gas - that hands the BoJ ammunition at Jul 31, where it also updates forecasts. Risk: an MoF strike snaps the yen higher in minutes; a hawkish BoJ breaks the carry.

catalysts.event: 10-day ceasefire proposal - mediators float lifting Hormuz restrictions
catalysts.note: The single biggest two-way risk on the board: acceptance unwinds the oil premium and flips CAD, USOIL, gold and the whole risk complex in a session. The White House has not accepted; diplomacy is proceeding.

catalysts.event: US strikes - 10th consecutive night; Hormuz traffic sharply reduced
catalysts.note: Iran fired on commercial vessels over the weekend (four intercepted); the US naval blockade of Iranian ports is in force; every headline moves oil, CAD, JPY and gold.
Spot ~162.5, above the flagged line at a 40-year low; verbal warnings only so far; the record 11.73tn yen Apr/May precedent failed to hold within six weeks.
Services 3.7% follow-through decides the BoE Jul 30 dissent count and the near-term cable direction.
Unemployment has drifted to 4.4% - a further rise kills the Aug 11 RBA hike tail.
Hold at 2.25% is ~88% priced; the September-hike signal is the entire trade for EUR.
The AUD's first domestic catalyst in weeks; decides the Aug 11 RBA and flips the AUD/NZD asymmetry if hot.
Warsh round 2; a hold is expected; Sept-hike odds ~55-60% and oil-dependent - the tone is the tell.
Dissent count after services 3.7%, plus new forecasts.
Tokyo trend CPI at 1.9% gives the hawks ammunition with spot above the MoF line.
Q2 CPI on Jul 29 decides it; loosening labour argues caution.
The oil pass-through test for the Fed's September call.
Live for a back-to-back hike after the 4.1% Q2 print - unless officials discount the fuel component.

geopolitics: For the first time in a week, the oil trade is two-sided. The war itself has not de-escalated: the US is carrying out its 10th consecutive night of strikes on Iran, Tehran fired on commercial vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz over the weekend (four intercepted), the US naval blockade of Iranian ports remains in force, and shipping through the strait - normally ~20% of world oil flow - is severely disrupted. Brent has run ~30% off its July lows to ~$90.3 and US pump prices are back at $4/gallon. But two things stalled the momentum. First, mediators floated a 10-day ceasefire that would explicitly include lifting the Hormuz restrictions in exchange for a return to negotiations; the White House stopped short of endorsing it but confirmed diplomacy is proceeding. Second, the price action itself went flat - WTI ~$82.4 (-0.1%), Brent unchanged - which is the market telling you the escalation is largely in the price and the marginal headline risk has flipped to the downside. FX transmission: (1) CAD loses its crown - still a net-energy exporter with a genuine tailwind, but June CPI cooled to 2.8% with all three BoC core gauges falling (trim 1.8%, median 1.9%, common 2.6%), and a ceasefire would remove the terms-of-trade premium outright; AUD/CAD neutralises to RANGE while NZD/CAD flips to LONG. (2) The USD's oil-inflation bid flattened with crude: DXY slipped to ~100.7 and the dollar lost ground to both Antipodeans; the Sept-hike tail (~55-60%) is intact but explicitly oil-dependent. (3) The JPY is still the loser but the offset is building - USD/JPY pushed through to ~162.5, above the MoF line at a 40-year low, yet the same oil shock is filtering into Tokyo CPI (core 1.6%, trend gauge 1.9%), handing the BoJ real ammunition at Jul 31. (4) The EUR flips from pure victim to conditional beneficiary: the energy resurgence that was read as a terms-of-trade drag is now the main argument for a September ECB hike. (5) Gold coils at $4,000 and silver leads (+0.8%) on its industrial leg. (6) NZD's hot CPI is itself an oil story - petrol +27.5%, ex-fuel CPI just 2.9% - which means a ceasefire would undercut the hawkish repricing at source. Every leg of today's map keys off the same binary: whether that 10-day ceasefire gets signed.
